Description: It represents the broadest geographical categorization, capturing large regions or overarching areas such as Africa & MENAP, Asia, Europe & Americas, and entries like "Un mapped" and "Memo." It serves as the primary grouping for geographical tracking, providing a macro view of location-based data across major global regions. This level is crucial for understanding the overall geographical distribution and segmentation of operations or activities

It provides a secondary layer of geographical categorization, breaking down the L0 regions into more specific areas such as AFRICA, MENAP (Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan, and Pakistan), ASA (Asia South Asia), GCNA (Greater China and North Asia), and Americas. It further segments the broad regions into distinct geographical areas, allowing for a finer analysis of location-based data. This level is essential for identifying specific countries or clusters within the larger regional divisions, including unique entries like SCB China and UK Consolidated.

It offers a third layer of geographical categorization, detailing the L1 areas into sub-regions like East Africa, Southern Africa, MENA, ASEAN, Greater China, and Europe. It breaks down the L1 categories into specialized geographical units, which can include particular countries or operational zones. This level is useful for detailed geographical analysis and for pinpointing specific areas within the broader regional context, such as North East Asia and Rest of MENA.

It provides details at individual countries or specific operational sites such as Nigeria, Japan, Kenya, UAE, and USA Bchs. This level is crucial for understanding the geographical distribution of entities and their associated operations, including countries like Uganda, Botswana, Mauritius, and South Africa.

It provides further segmentation of the L3 locations, often repeating the country name for clarity or specifying sub-regions like "Other Africa," "Bahrain DBU," and "ASEAN - Inp." It helps in identifying particular divisions or branches within the broader L3 categories, allowing for a finer analysis of location-based data. This level is essential for pinpointing specific operational zones or administrative divisions within countries, including areas like "Africa - Inp" and "Europe - Inp."

It offers a third layer of geographical categorization, detailing specific entities or branches within the L4 areas, such as "Sc Bank Nigeria Ltd," "Sc Securities (Kenya) Ltd," and "Sc Bank Japan." It breaks down the L4 categories into specialized units, which can include particular companies, financial institutions, or operational sites. This level is useful for detailed geographical analysis and for identifying specific business entities within the broader regional context, including entities like "Sc Bank Mauritius Ltd" and "Sc Bank Singapore Ltd."

The most granular level of geographical categorization, this column provides the finest breakdown of locations, often specifying operational status or administrative details such as "Country," "Group Adj," "Un-mapped," "Ventures," "Region," and "Head Office." It allows for precise tracking and management of location-based data, enabling organizations to monitor and control operations at the most detailed geographical level. This level is critical for project management, strategic planning, and operational accountability within specific entities or administrative categories, including entries like "SC GBS" and "Rubik."

This column categorizes data according to different business views, such as Corporate and Institutional Banking (CIB), Wealth and Retail Banking (WRB), and Financial Planning and Analysis (FPNA). It enables users to analyze data from various perspectives, aligning with specific business needs and strategies.
Primary key for this dimension. Its defined by concatenating of all other attributes in the dimension
